title: Show filled red heart for favorited events
description:
The FavoriteButton renders the same outline heart regardless of whether the event is favorited. Users have no visual feedback about current favorite state. We need to thread isFavorited state from the DB through the route loaders down to the button, and show a filled red heart when favorited.

What to build:
- Update list route (camera-events.index.tsx): call getFavoritedEventIdsFn in loader alongside loadEvents, pass favoriteEventIds to CameraEventsListPage
- Update detail route (camera-events..tsx): call getIsFavoritedFn in loader alongside loadEvent, pass isFavorited to CameraEventDetailPage
- Update CameraEventsListPage props to accept favoriteEventIds:string[], pass isFavorited={favoriteEventIds.includes(event.id)} to each EventCard FavoriteButton
- Update CameraEventDetailPage props to accept isFavorited:boolean, pass to FavoriteButton
- Update FavoriteButton: add isFavorited prop, use useState for optimistic toggle on click, render Heart with fill='currentColor' and text-red-500 when favorited
- On click: optimistically flip local state, call toggleFavoriteFn, update from server response isFavorited field
- TDD: update FavoriteButton tests for filled/outline states and optimistic toggle
acceptance_criteria: Favorited events show a filled red heart. Unfavorited events show an outline heart. Clicking a favorited event's heart removes it (heart goes outline). Clicking an unfavorited event's heart adds it (heart goes filled red). Optimistic update is immediate — no waiting for server. State is sourced from DB on page load.

title: Wire favorites store into server functions
description:
Once the favorites store exists, the server action and loaders need to use it. Currently toggleFavoriteFn discards all input and returns {ok:true}. The list and detail route loaders only fetch Frigate events — they have no concept of favorite state.

What to build:
- Update handleToggleFavorite in favorites.ts to call getFavoritesStore(), check current state, INSERT or DELETE, and return {ok:true,isFavorited:boolean}
- Update toggleFavoriteFn return type accordingly
- Add getFavoritedEventIdsFn: createServerFn GET that calls requireSession() + store.getFavoritedEventIds(userId), returns string[]
- Add getIsFavoritedFn: createServerFn GET with inputValidator for eventId, calls requireSession() + store.isFavorited(userId, eventId), returns boolean
- TDD: update existing favorites.test.ts, add tests for new server functions mocking the store
acceptance_criteria: toggleFavoriteFn returns isFavorited:true after first call, isFavorited:false after second call on same eventId. getFavoritedEventIdsFn returns array of favorited IDs for the session user. getIsFavoritedFn returns correct boolean. All three require session. All tested.
close_reason: handleToggleFavorite now accepts FavoritesStore, performs real toggle (INSERT OR IGNORE / DELETE), returns isFavorited boolean. handleGetFavoritedEventIds and handleGetIsFavorited added as pure helpers. Three createServerFn wrappers (toggleFavoriteFn, getFavoritedEventIdsFn, getIsFavoritedFn) each call requireSession() first. 13 tests passing.

title: Create favorites SQLite store
description:
The toggle server action has no persistence — it validates and discards. We need a favorites-store.ts following the push-store.ts singleton pattern to persist favorite_events to SQLite. This is the data layer that all other favorites work depends on.

What to build:
- src/features/camera-events/server/favorites-store.ts
- Table: favorite_events (user_id TEXT, event_id TEXT, created_at INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY (user_id, event_id))
- Open DB in WAL mode via openSqlite('data/camera-events.db')
- Pre-compile statements in a stmts object (same pattern as push-store.ts)
- Methods: addFavorite(userId, eventId), removeFavorite(userId, eventId), isFavorited(userId, eventId): boolean, getFavoritedEventIds(userId): string[]
- Lazy singleton via getFavoritesStore()
- Schema migration runs on first call (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS)
- TDD: write tests first covering all methods, including edge cases (double-add, remove non-existent)
acceptance_criteria: getFavoritesStore() returns a store with addFavorite, removeFavorite, isFavorited, getFavoritedEventIds. Table is created on first call. isFavorited returns false for unknown IDs. addFavorite is idempotent (double-add does not error). removeFavorite on non-existent entry does not error. All methods tested.
close_reason: favorites-store.ts created following push-store.ts pattern. CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS favorite_events (user_id, event_id, created_at, PRIMARY KEY). INSERT OR IGNORE for idempotent add, DELETE for remove, prepared statements, WAL mode, lazy singleton. 14 tests passing.

title: Add empty toggleFavorite server action for camera events
description: The favorite button on camera events pages needs a server action to call when clicked. The action should be a createServerFn handler that calls requireSession() and accepts an event ID, but performs no side effects for now. It exists as a placeholder until the favorites persistence layer is designed.
acceptance_criteria: toggleFavorite server action exists, calls requireSession() as first operation, accepts a valid event ID, and returns without error. No persistence logic yet.
close_reason: Pure handleToggleFavorite function + toggleFavoriteFn server action created in src/features/camera-events/server/favorites.ts. Calls requireSession(), validates event ID with isValidEventId(), returns { ok: true } as placeholder. 4 tests passing.

title: Add favorite heart icon button to camera events pages
description: Users have no way to mark individual camera events as favorites. A Lucide heart icon button should appear after the event title on both the event list and event detail pages. Clicking it should call a toggleFavorite server action. The button must be accessible and keyboard-navigable.
acceptance_criteria: Heart icon button renders after event title on /camera-events list and /camera-events/:id detail pages. Button is visually distinct and accessible. Clicking the button invokes the toggleFavorite server action.
close_reason: FavoriteButton component added (Heart icon, aria-label, calls toggleFavoriteFn on click). Placed after the h1 title on the detail page and as an absolute-positioned sibling to MediaCard in the list page EventCard (avoids nested interactive elements). 3 tests added.
